desires: To force teammate_A to acknowledge the boundary I set and stop dumping unfinished work on me, so our shared future isn't ruined by their negligence.
To regain a sliver of energy before my sleep debt crushes me completely, because if I collapse now, I can't protect the relationship I value most.
To feel confident that my assertiveness was justified and didn't come across as uncooperative, ensuring my trust with the group remains intact despite this conflict.

goals.description: Send the final, unambiguous message to teammate_A confirming the timeline renegotiation and explicitly stating that their share remains their responsibility, ending the negotiation phase immediately.
goals.rationale: My 'affiliation' value demands I protect the relationship with cohort_3 by resolving this conflict now so it doesn't bleed into our dinner. My 'assertive' axis requires me to set a hard boundary against taking on their work despite my zero energy; delaying this message risks me slipping into guilt-tripping or doing the work out of fear, which would destroy my long-term planning horizon and professional integrity.

goals.description: Execute a strict 45-minute digital detox (no phone, no email) followed by a low-stimulus meal with cohort_3 to physically recharge and restore social energy reserves before sleep debt becomes critical.
goals.rationale: With only 2 hours of sleep debt and high stress, my physical stamina is at risk. Prioritizing health and leisure now is an investment in my ability to maintain relationships later. If I burn out tonight, I cannot fulfill my core value of affiliation tomorrow.

goals.description: Review the sent message one last time mentally to ensure the tone was firm but not aggressive, accepting that some friction is necessary for clarity rather than trying to soften the blow.
goals.rationale: I need confidence that my assertiveness was justified. However, since I have already sent the draft, obsessing over the wording now adds unnecessary cognitive load. I will defer this reflection until after I have recharged, trusting that the directness aligns with my values.

agent.last_reasoning: I must send this final message to end the negotiation phase and protect my boundaries, despite zero energy. Delaying risks guilt-tripping myself into doing their work, which violates my long-term planning horizon. This resolves g1 immediately so I can focus on recharging for cohort_3 later.

goals.description: Draft and send a firm, direct text to teammate_A refusing their request immediately, citing my own deadline and our established trust boundaries.
goals.rationale: My value priority for relationships (0.5) demands I protect the trust we've built rather than enabling bad behavior that will erode it. My assertive conflict style requires me to say 'no' directly now, not later.

goals.description: Focus exclusively on completing my own share of the project by 7 PM, blocking all other inputs until done.
goals.rationale: Meeting my commitment to the group is essential for maintaining our working relationship. With only 30% progress made, I need deliberate focus to finish without distractions before my deadline.

goals.description: Prepare a strict plan for tomorrow's morning routine including an alarm and no-sleep-debt recovery steps.
goals.rationale: While avoiding the crash is important (health priority 0.13), my long planning horizon suggests I should secure this boundary first. Once the immediate crisis with Teammate A is handled, I can schedule the sleep recovery protocol.

desires: Secure my boundary with Teammate A immediately so they stop expecting me to do their work and I don't lose the trust we've built.
Finish my own share of the project by 7 PM without any distractions, ensuring I meet my commitment to the group.
Avoid the physical crash tomorrow morning caused by ignoring the 2 hours of sleep debt I already have.
